question: In paragraph 3, what does the word 'electrified' mean?
correctText: Filled with great excitement

rewrites.newText: Given a painful shock by electric current
Made furious and resentful by the discovery
Left puzzled and unable to understand the find

question: In paragraph 5, what does 'undeterred' mean?
correctText: Not discouraged or put off

rewrites.newText: Not interested in continuing her fossil work
Not properly trained or taught in science
Not believed or trusted by the scientific community

question: In paragraph 8, what does 'teetered on the edge of financial ruin' suggest?
correctText: The family was dangerously close to having no money at all

rewrites.currentText: The family was saving money carefully
rewrites.newText: The family was carefully setting aside money to protect their future security

rewrites.currentText: The family lived on a cliff edge
rewrites.newText: The family literally lived in a cottage perched on the crumbling cliff edge

rewrites.currentText: The family had recently lost some money
rewrites.newText: The family had recently lost a small amount of money in a bad deal

rewrites.currentText: The family was about to become very wealthy
rewrites.newText: The family stood on the brink of suddenly becoming extremely rich and comfortable for life

question: In paragraph 10, what does the word 'elusive' mean in 'public recognition remained elusive'?
correctText: Difficult to obtain or find

rewrites.newText: Not needed or important to her
Something she actively did not want to receive
Quickly lost from people's memory over the years

question: In paragraph 2, what does the word 'consortium' mean?
correctText: A group of companies working together

rewrites.newText: A department run by the national government
A large fine charged for breaking the rules

question: In paragraph 7, what does 'nerve-wracking' mean?
correctText: Causing great anxiety or tension

rewrites.newText: Leaving the body completely worn out

question: In paragraph 3, what does 'bioluminescence' mean?
correctText: The ability to produce light from within the body

rewrites.currentText: The ability to see in darkness
rewrites.newText: The ability to see clearly in the complete darkness of the deep

rewrites.currentText: The ability to change shape underwater
rewrites.newText: The ability to change the body's shape to escape from hungry predators

rewrites.currentText: The ability to glow when touched
rewrites.newText: The ability to light up only at the moment of being touched

rewrites.currentText: The ability to detect heat from other creatures
rewrites.newText: The ability to sense the body heat given off by other nearby creatures

question: In paragraph 4, what does 'captivated' mean?
correctText: Fascinated and held the attention of

rewrites.newText: Filled with fear and a desire to flee
Physically caught and held prisoner against its will
Left confused and unable to make any sense of it

question: In paragraph 5, what does the word 'sobering' suggest about the discovery of the plastic bag?
correctText: It made people think seriously about a worrying issue

rewrites.newText: It was a thrilling and completely unexpected surprise for everyone

rewrites.currentText: It helped scientists understand deep-sea pressure
rewrites.newText: It helped scientists better understand the crushing pressure of the deep

question: In paragraph 5, what does 'haunting' mean when describing the plaster casts?
correctText: Deeply moving and impossible to forget

rewrites.newText: Highly useful to researchers studying the disaster

question: In paragraph 10, what does 'intricate' mean?
correctText: Containing very fine, detailed patterns

rewrites.newText: Extremely old and dating from ancient times
Very large and covering a great area
Made from rare and very costly precious materials
Concealed underneath the floor and completely out of sight

question: In paragraph 1, what does 'dread' mean?
correctText: Great fear or anxiety about something

rewrites.newText: A thrilling sense of eager and happy anticipation
A strong and eager desire to explore something new
A heavy feeling of being completely physically worn out

question: In paragraph 4, what does 'translucent' mean?
correctText: Allowing some light through but not fully transparent

rewrites.newText: Perfectly clear and easy to see straight through completely
Brightly sparkling and shining with many different bright rainbow colours

question: In paragraph 5, what does 'fissure' mean?
correctText: A narrow opening or crack in rock

rewrites.newText: A deep pool of water at the base

question: In paragraph 12, what does 'unpersuasive' mean in the phrase 'the sensible thing felt remarkably unpersuasive'?
correctText: Not convincing or compelling

rewrites.newText: Extremely convincing and hard to resist
Confusing and very difficult to understand
Extremely dangerous and full of serious risk
Completely impossible to actually carry out in practice

question: In paragraph 1, what does 'silhouette' mean?
correctText: A dark outline seen against a lighter background

rewrites.newText: A dark shadow cast onto the ground by sunlight
A photograph taken of the old tower at very close range
A particular style of tall stone building found along the coast

question: In paragraph 8, what does 'decrepit' mean?
correctText: In a very poor condition due to age and neglect

rewrites.newText: Rising to an extremely great and impressive height above the surrounding rocks
Freshly painted and looking bright, clean and in completely new condition again

question: In paragraph 5, what does 'complacent' mean?
correctText: Satisfied to the point of not recognising danger

rewrites.newText: Deeply frightened and constantly afraid of an approaching attack

question: In paragraph 12, what does 'defiance' mean?
correctText: Bold resistance and refusal to obey

rewrites.newText: Calm and quiet acceptance of being ruled
Great physical strength and raw power used in battle
Complete agreement with the wishes of those in authority
A deep and lasting fear of those who hold great power

question: In paragraph 2, what does 'reckless' mean?
correctText: Acting without thinking about consequences

rewrites.newText: Genuinely brave and physically strong in danger
Lazy and showing no interest in anything
Clever and cunning but not at all honest

question: In paragraph 1, what does 'enduring' mean in 'as enduring' as the tale?
correctText: Lasting for a very long time

rewrites.newText: Only found or discovered a short time ago
Painful and difficult to get through or bear

question: In paragraph 6, what does 'controversy' mean?
correctText: Prolonged public disagreement

rewrites.newText: Great excitement and eager enthusiasm
